description: This diagram illustrates the process of merging two adjacent polygons into a single, unified polygon. On the left, two distinct, contiguous polygons, labelled "1" and "2", are shown. Both polygons are depicted with a light green stippled fill and a light blue outline, set against a light brown background. An orange arrow, labelled "Merge", points from these initial polygons to a single resulting polygon on the right. The final state shows a single, larger polygon, labelled "3", which represents the combined spatial extent of the original polygons "1" and "2". This resulting polygon maintains the same light green stippled fill and light blue outline. This operation is common in Geographic Information System (GIS) data processing for tasks such as land cover generalization.
